text: The surgeon performs a stapling approach on the colon, which is visibly rolling away during the procedure.
The surgeon elevates the colon, which rolls away at an angle, causing a non-perpendicular staple firing. The surgeon readjusts the stapler to ensure proper alignment before elevating the bowel again.
The surgeon adjusts the colon to ensure it falls away, achieving a perpendicular staple line with good perfusion to the planned anastomosis site.
The surgeon checks the tip of the colon to ensure it has fallen down perpendicularly for stapling. The colon is aligned correctly, allowing the surgeon to fire the stapler without any issues.
The surgeon performs a second firing of the stapler on the bowel to ensure a flat staple line, aligning it perpendicular to the original staple line.
The surgeon successfully prevents the colon from rolling away, achieving a perpendicular staple line that avoids curving and narrowing the lumen during the ileocolic resection.
